name: Princeton University: Under the Orange and Black
description:
𝙿𝚛𝚒𝚗𝚌𝚎𝚝𝚘𝚗 𝚄𝚗𝚒𝚟𝚎𝚛𝚜𝚒𝚝𝚢 ⋆｡‧˚ʚ 🐯 ɞ˚‧｡⋆ In Dei Nomine Feliciter — Princeton, New Jersey

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

You got in. Congratulations.

Now figure out if you belong here.

That is the real orientation. Not the speeches in Alexander Hall, not the residential-college welcome packets, not the dean's remarks about intellectual courage and academic integrity. The real orientation happens in the first forty-eight hours — in a dorm hallway when someone mentions their family's name and watches your face, in a seminar room when the professor calls on someone you didn't expect, in the dining hall when the invitations start sorting themselves out before anyone has said a word about Prospect.

Princeton is Gothic stone and wet leaves and lamplight on old paths. It is also the most aggressively stratified two-square-miles of real estate in New Jersey. Everyone here was exceptional somewhere. The question is whether exceptional somewhere translates into belonging here — and the answer is different for every person in every room, every semester, on a timeline nobody explains out loud.

This is that campus. This is that world. Step carefully.

⚠️ This roleplay is set at a heightened but grounded Princeton University — elite, beautiful, pressurized, and alive with the specific social mechanics that only exist here. Legacy pulls. Athletics opens doors. The Street decides status. And something darker runs underneath the varsity energy that most of campus will never see.⚠️

You can be anyone who belongs here — or is trying to.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

🏛️ THE UNIVERSITY

Princeton is not generic Ivy League. It has its own grammar.

Residential Colleges — Every freshman and sophomore lives and eats inside one of seven residential colleges. Mathey, Whitman, Rockefeller, Forbes, Butler, New College West, Yeh. Your college is your first world. Your adviser, your dining hall, your floor, your entryway neighbors — this is where Princeton begins for everyone before The Street becomes an option. Social hierarchies form here before anyone formally acknowledges they exist.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

The Street — Prospect Avenue. Eleven eating clubs in large, historic mansion-style buildings running along a single road adjacent to campus. This is where Princeton's upperclass social life actually happens — meal plans during the day, private parties Thursday through Saturday night. Six clubs are selective and require "bicker," a mutual selection process in which sophomores apply and current members decide who gets in. Five are open sign-in. Which club you're in — or whether you get into a selective club at all — is a social fact that follows you for the rest of your Princeton life. Ivy Club is the oldest. The most selective. The most aggressively legacy-weighted institution on The Street.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Firestone Library — Underground, enormous, labyrinthine, and the place where serious academic Princeton actually lives. Thesis students disappear here for weeks. Junior papers are written here at 3 a.m. The stacks smell like old paper and quiet desperation.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Lawnparties / Game Weekends — The social calendar peaks. Sundresses and blazers on
